attrs.alt: Entitlement Management: access package request flow from requestor through approval stages to automatic access grant and expiry

content.text: What Entitlement Management Solves

Entitlement Management is an Entra ID Governance feature that lets administrators define 'access packages' — bundles of resources (groups, app roles, SharePoint sites, Teams) that a user or external guest can request as a single unit. Instead of managing individual resource assignments for each employee or partner, you define what a role needs, who can request it, who approves, and when access expires.

Without Entitlement Management: provisioning a new contractor requires a ticket to IT, manual group membership, manual app role assignment, manual SharePoint permission, and a separate reminder ticket to revoke access 3 months later.

With Entitlement Management: the contractor visits myaccess.microsoft.com, requests the 'Contractor Access' package, their manager approves in one click, all resources are assigned automatically, and access expires on the configured date with no follow-up required.
B2B guest users: external partners can request access packages without needing a pre-existing account. Entitlement Management creates an Entra B2B guest invitation as part of the access request flow.
Core Concepts
Concept
Description
Catalog
A container that groups related access packages and their resources. Ownership of a catalog can be delegated to a business unit owner without giving them admin rights over the whole tenant.
Access Package
A named bundle of resource roles. One package can include memberships in multiple groups, app roles across different applications, and SharePoint site permissions.
Policy

Rules attached to an access package: who can request it (specific users, all members, guests, specific connected organizations), who approves (manager, specific people, resource owner), how long access lasts, and whether periodic access reviews are required.
Resource Role
The specific role or permission within a resource: a group membership, a particular app role, or a SharePoint site access level.
Connected Organization
An external Entra tenant or identity domain whose users can be allowed to request access packages. Defines which B2B guest principals are trusted requestors.
Access Package Assignment
The live record of a user holding access from a package. Includes start date, end date, and which policy granted it.
myaccess.microsoft.com
The self-service portal where end users browse available packages, submit requests, check request status, and extend or return access.
Creating a Catalog

A catalog scopes which resources and packages a delegated catalog owner can manage. The catalog owner does not need the Global Administrator or Identity Governance Administrator role — they only need the Catalog Owner role assignment for that specific catalog.

# Requires EntitlementManagement.ReadWrite.All
# Create a catalog for the Engineering division
$body = @{
    displayName        = 'Engineering Resources'
    description        = 'Resources for engineering team members and contractors'
    isExternallyVisible = $false
} | ConvertTo-Json

$catalog = Invoke-MgGraphRequest -Method POST \
  -Uri 'https://graph.microsoft.com/v1.0/identityGovernance/entitlementManagement/catalogs' \
  -Body $body -ContentType 'application/json'

Write-Host "Catalog ID: $($catalog.id)"

# Add a resource to the catalog (an Entra group)
$groupBody = @{
    catalogId    = $catalog.id
    requestType  = 'adminAdd'
    resource     = @{
        originSystem = 'AadGroup'
        originId     = '<group-object-id>'
    }
} | ConvertTo-Json -Depth 5

Invoke-MgGraphRequest -Method POST \
  -Uri 'https://graph.microsoft.com/v1.0/identityGovernance/entitlementManagement/resourceRequests' \
  -Body $groupBody -ContentType 'application/json'
Creating an Access Package

An access package lives inside a catalog and defines what a requestor receives. You can add multiple resource roles — a group for email distribution, an app role for a SaaS application, and a SharePoint site — to one package.
Defining a Policy

A policy specifies who can request the package, how many approval stages are required, and how long access lasts. One access package can have multiple policies — for example, one policy for internal employees (self-approve, 365-day duration) and a separate policy for external guests (manager approval, 90-day duration).
Expiration Types
expiration.type
Behavior
afterDuration
Access expires after a fixed period (e.g., P90D = 90 days) from assignment date
afterDateTime
Access expires on a specific date/time regardless of when granted (useful for project-based access)
noExpiration
Access does not expire automatically — reviewer or admin must remove it explicitly
Avoid noExpiration for external guests or high-privilege roles. Always pair it with mandatory periodic access reviews if you must use it.
Administering Direct Assignments

Admins can assign an access package to a user directly, bypassing the request and approval flow. This is useful for initial onboarding or for migrating existing access into the entitlement management system.

# Direct admin assignment — no approval flow
$assignBody = @{
    requestType     = 'adminAdd'
    accessPackageAssignment = @{
        targetId        = '<user-object-id>'
        assignmentPolicyId = '<policy-id>'
        accessPackageId = $pkg.id
    }
} | ConvertTo-Json -Depth 5

Invoke-MgGraphRequest -Method POST \
  -Uri 'https://graph.microsoft.com/v1.0/identityGovernance/entitlementManagement/assignmentRequests' \
  -Body $assignBody -ContentType 'application/json'

# List all current assignments for a package
$assignments = Invoke-MgGraphRequest -Method GET \
  -Uri "https://graph.microsoft.com/v1.0/identityGovernance/entitlementManagement/assignments?`$filter=accessPackageId eq '$($pkg.id)'&`$expand=target"

$assignments.value | Select-Object @{N='User';E={$_.target.email}}, state,
    @{N='Expires';E={$_.schedule.expiration.endDateTime}}
Connected Organizations for External Access

A connected organization represents an external Entra tenant or an identity domain (for social or non-Entra identities) whose users are allowed to request access packages. When an external user submits a request, Entitlement Management creates or matches an Entra B2B guest account automatically.

# Add a connected organization (partner tenant by domain)
$connBody = @{
    displayName = 'Fabrikam Partner'
    description = 'Authorized partner for project Nightfall'
    identitySources = @(
        @{
            '@odata.type' = '#microsoft.graph.domainIdentitySource'
            domainName    = 'fabrikam.com'
            displayName   = 'fabrikam.com'
        }
    )
    state = 'configured'
} | ConvertTo-Json -Depth 5

$conn = Invoke-MgGraphRequest -Method POST \
  -Uri 'https://graph.microsoft.com/v1.0/identityGovernance/entitlementManagement/connectedOrganizations' \
  -Body $connBody -ContentType 'application/json'

# Now create a policy on the same package that targets this connected org
# In requestorSettings: scopeType = "AllConfiguredConnectedOrganizationUsers"
# or scopeType = "SpecificConnectedOrganizationUsers" with connectedOrganizationId

Setting isExternallyVisible = true on a catalog makes it discoverable in myaccess.microsoft.com by guest users who don't have direct links. Keep it false unless you want any authenticated guest to browse your catalog.
Separation of Duties Across Incompatible Packages

Entitlement Management supports incompatible access package enforcement. If your SOD policy says that a user cannot hold both 'Finance Approver' and 'Finance Submitter', you can mark those packages as incompatible — requests that would create the conflict are blocked automatically.

# Mark two access packages as incompatible with each other
$pkgA_id = '<finance-approver-package-id>'
$pkgB_id = '<finance-submitter-package-id>'

# Add pkgB as incompatible with pkgA
Invoke-MgGraphRequest -Method POST \
  -Uri "https://graph.microsoft.com/v1.0/identityGovernance/entitlementManagement/accessPackages/$pkgA_id/incompatibleAccessPackages/`$ref" \
  -Body (@{ '@odata.id' = "https://graph.microsoft.com/v1.0/identityGovernance/entitlementManagement/accessPackages/$pkgB_id" } | ConvertTo-Json) \
  -ContentType 'application/json'

# Now a user who holds an assignment in pkgA cannot request pkgB,
# and vice versa. The request is rejected at submission.
IaC with Terraform
The AzureAD Terraform provider supports Entitlement Management resources. Codifying catalogs and packages enables repeatable, auditable governance setup.
Auditing Entitlement Management

All entitlement management events (access requests, approvals, denials, assignments, expirations) appear in the Entra audit log under the service name 'Entitlement Management'. Query these with KQL for compliance reporting.

// All access package requests in the last 30 days
AuditLogs
| where TimeGenerated > ago(30d)
| where LoggedByService == 'Entitlement Management'
| extend User    = tostring(InitiatedBy.user.userPrincipalName)
| extend Package = tostring(TargetResources[0].displayName)
| extend Result  = ResultDescription
| project TimeGenerated, User, Package, OperationName, Result
| order by TimeGenerated desc

// Denied requests (rejected by approver or system)
AuditLogs
| where LoggedByService == 'Entitlement Management'
| where OperationName == 'Request denied'
| extend Requestor = tostring(InitiatedBy.user.userPrincipalName)
| extend Package   = tostring(TargetResources[0].displayName)
| extend Reason    = tostring(AdditionalDetails[?(@.key=='denial_reason')].value)
| project TimeGenerated, Requestor, Package, Reason
Summary

Entitlement Management replaces manual provisioning with self-service request workflows: users visit myaccess.microsoft.com, select an access package, and receive all resources it contains after approval.
Access packages bundle multiple resource roles (groups, app roles, SharePoint sites) into a single requestable unit, reducing administrative overhead and ensuring consistent provisioning.

Policies define who can request (internal users, specific groups, external partners via connected organizations), how many approval stages are required, how long access lasts, and whether periodic reviews are required.
Connected organizations let external partner users request access via B2B — Entitlement Management creates or matches a guest account automatically without requiring pre-staging.
Incompatible access packages enforce separation of duties: a user who holds one package is blocked from requesting another.
All events surface to the Entra audit log as 'Entitlement Management' service entries, enabling KQL-based compliance reporting.
